punchline: So now I have two mortgages.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that an attempt to solve, escape, or improve a situation just doubled the original financial burden instead of relieving it.

---

JOKE 1:
My therapist said I needed to stop worrying about money and invest in myself. So now I have two mortgages.

---

JOKE 2:
My wife and I were fighting constantly, so we agreed some space would be good for our marriage. So now I have two mortgages.

---

JOKE 3:
I told my financial advisor I was drowning in my mortgage payments and needed a way out. He said the best move was to buy a rental property and let the income cover both. So now I have two mortgages.
selected_joke:
JOKE 2:
My wife and I were fighting constantly, so we agreed some space would be good for our marriage. So now I have two mortgages.

punchline: And that's why I'm no longer allowed at the aquarium.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that a seemingly innocent or reasonable action was interpreted in a wildly inappropriate way by the aquarium, turning mundane behavior into something banworthy.

---

JOKE 1:
The sign next to the tank said "Please Touch." So I reached in and pet one. Apparently "Please Touch" was the name of the stingray. And that's why I'm no longer allowed at the aquarium.

---

JOKE 2:
I like to support local businesses, so when I saw the aquarium had a fish called a Red Snapper, I told the guy at the front desk I'd take two pounds of it. And that's why I'm no longer allowed at the aquarium.

---

JOKE 3:
My therapist said I need to stop bottling up my emotions and try releasing them into the wild. So I brought a bag of goldfish and dumped them into the shark tank. I whispered "be free" and everything. And that's why I'm no longer allowed at the aquarium.
selected_joke:
JOKE 2:
I like to support local businesses, so when I saw the aquarium had a fish called a Red Snapper, I told the guy at the front desk I'd take two pounds of it. And that's why I'm no longer allowed at the aquarium.

punchline: Turns out it was just a regular dog.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline deflates an expectation of something extraordinary or dangerous by revealing the subject was mundane all along — the setup must build a frame where a normal dog is mistaken for something remarkable.

---

JOKE 1:
My neighbor warned me he had a purebred Czech Wolfdog, imported from Eastern Europe, trained by former military handlers, with papers going back six generations. I was terrified to go near his yard for months. Finally met the thing. Turns out it was just a regular dog.

---

JOKE 2:
A psychic told me I'd be visited by a powerful spirit animal that would change the course of my life forever. I spent weeks meditating, burning sage, preparing myself spiritually. Then one morning something appeared on my porch and stared deep into my soul. Turns out it was just a regular dog.

---

JOKE 3:
The government cordoned off three blocks near my apartment. Hazmat suits, helicopters, the whole thing. A reporter said an unidentified creature had been spotted in the alley and they couldn't rule out anything. I stayed glued to the news for six hours. Turns out it was just a regular dog.
selected_joke:
JOKE 3:
The government cordoned off three blocks near my apartment. Hazmat suits, helicopters, the whole thing. A reporter said an unidentified creature had been spotted in the alley and they couldn't rule out anything. I stayed glued to the news for six hours. Turns out it was just a regular dog.

punchline: He said, 'That's not what I meant by cashback.'
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline flips a financial/transactional term ("cashback") from its normal retail meaning to a literal or inappropriate interpretation — someone physically returning cash, stealing, or doing something absurd with actual cash.

---

JOKE 1:
I robbed a bank and felt guilty, so the next day I went back and slipped the money through the slot. The teller hit the alarm and the cops showed up. I couldn't believe it. I was just trying to return it. The manager tackled me to the ground. He said, "That's not what I meant by cashback."

---

JOKE 2:
My buddy works at a grocery store. A customer paid for his items, then just stood there holding out his hand. My buddy peeled a twenty off his own wallet and handed it to the guy. The manager pulled him aside. He said, "That's not what I meant by cashback."

---

JOKE 3:
I started a job at a casino and on my first day the pit boss told me to focus on cashback for our loyal customers. So I went around the blackjack tables stuffing twenties into people's back pockets. Got through about nine people before security grabbed me. The pit boss was furious. He said, "That's not what I meant by cashback."
selected_joke:
JOKE 2:
My buddy works at a grocery store. A customer paid for his items, then just stood there holding out his hand. My buddy peeled a twenty off his own wallet and handed it to the guy. The manager pulled him aside. He said, "That's not what I meant by cashback."

punchline: I didn't even know you could return a tattoo.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline treats a tattoo — something famously permanent and irreversible — as a casual retail purchase with a return policy.

---

JOKE 1:
My ex said she was getting rid of the tattoo of my name on her arm. I said good luck with the laser removal. She said no, she's just taking it back to the shop with the receipt. I didn't even know you could return a tattoo.

---

JOKE 2:
My buddy walked into the tattoo parlor looking upset, and the artist said, "No worries, we have a 30-day satisfaction guarantee." I didn't even know you could return a tattoo.

---

JOKE 3:
I saw a sign at a tattoo shop that said "No Refunds." Which honestly raised more questions than it answered, because I didn't even know you could return a tattoo.
selected_joke:
JOKE 3:
I saw a sign at a tattoo shop that said "No Refunds." Which honestly raised more questions than it answered, because I didn't even know you could return a tattoo.

punchline: Apparently that's not what 'bring your own bag' means.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals the speaker interpreted a store/event policy literally or in a wildly different context than intended, exposing their oblivious misunderstanding.

---

JOKE 1:

I got kicked out of the grocery store for showing up with a pillowcase. Apparently that's not what "bring your own bag" means.

---

JOKE 2:

I thought the wine festival was going to be way more fun. I showed up with a suitcase and started filling it with bottles off every table. Apparently that's not what "bring your own bag" means.

---

JOKE 3:

My wife told me I should come to her yoga class — they encourage you to bring your own bag. So I walked in with a duffel full of Doritos and sat in the back. Apparently that's not what "bring your own bag" means.
selected_joke: I got kicked out of the grocery store for showing up with a pillowcase. Apparently that's not what "bring your own bag" means.

punchline: The worst part is, the horse was right.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that the speaker is genuinely conceding a point to a horse, as if the humiliation isn't the horse talking or having opinions, but that its criticism was valid.

---

JOKE 1:
A horse once told me I'd never amount to anything. That was fifteen years ago, and I've thought about it every single day since. Not because a horse spoke to me — I've made my peace with that. The worst part is, the horse was right.

---

JOKE 2:
I got into an argument with a horse about whether I was in any shape to ride him. They had to pull us apart. Security reviewed the tape, talked to witnesses, and I was asked to leave the stable. The worst part is, the horse was right.

---

JOKE 3:
My therapist suggested I try equine therapy. First session, the horse just looked at me for a long time, then slowly backed away. My therapist said that was a significant emotional breakthrough and we should discuss what it brought up for me. The worst part is, the horse was right.
selected_joke:
JOKE 2:
I got into an argument with a horse about whether I was in any shape to ride him. They had to pull us apart. Security reviewed the tape, talked to witnesses, and I was asked to leave the stable. The worst part is, the horse was right.

punchline: And the landlord said, 'That's not a pet deposit.'
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that what someone assumed was a fee for keeping an animal was actually something else entirely — reframing an innocent housing situation into something darker, weirder, or more absurd.

---

JOKE 1:
I told my landlord I'd be moving in with my cat and asked if the $500 pet deposit was refundable. He looked confused. He said he didn't even know I had a cat. And the landlord said, "That's not a pet deposit."

---

JOKE 2:
My buddy was apartment hunting and found a place that required $300 upfront, no questions asked. He figured that was reasonable for having a dog. Showed up on move-in day with a golden retriever and a check. And the landlord said, "That's not a pet deposit."

---

JOKE 3:
I was reading my lease and saw a $1,000 charge labeled "deposit for creature on premises." I thought, well, that's a steep price for a hamster, but fair enough. I called to negotiate it down. And the landlord said, "That's not a pet deposit."
selected_joke:
JOKE 3:
I was reading my lease and saw a $1,000 charge labeled "deposit for creature on premises." I thought, well, that's a steep price for a hamster, but fair enough. I called to negotiate it down. And the landlord said, "That's not a pet deposit."

punchline: So technically, I'm employee of the month.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reframes a pathetic or disqualifying situation as an achievement by exploiting the technicality of being the only one in a category.

---

JOKE 1:
I work from home and I live alone. My boss said they're recognizing the top performer in every household. So technically, I'm employee of the month.

---

JOKE 2:
Everyone at my company got laid off last Friday. I only survived because I was on vacation and they couldn't reach me. So technically, I'm employee of the month.

---

JOKE 3:
I started my own business. No revenue, no customers, I just sit there. But there's nobody outperforming me. So technically, I'm employee of the month.
selected_joke:

punchline: They said it was the most polite robbery they'd ever seen.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that something intended as normal, helpful, or courteous behavior was accidentally perceived as a criminal act — or that an actual robbery was conducted with such excessive politeness that it confused everyone.

---

JOKE 1:
I'm Canadian and I moved to New York. First week, I held the door open for someone, apologized for being in their way, and asked if they could spare some change for the tip jar I thought I saw. They said it was the most polite robbery they'd ever seen.

---

JOKE 2:
I tried to return something at a store without a receipt. I said "I'm so sorry to bother you, I know this is a hassle, but if it's not too much trouble, could I please have the cash back? Again, I'm really sorry, and thank you so much for your time." They said it was the most polite robbery they'd ever seen.

---

JOKE 3:
My mom always taught me that manners cost nothing. So when I walked into the bank with a ski mask on because my face was cold, sneezed into my elbow, said "excuse me," and then handed the teller a note asking her to please have a wonderful day, I didn't understand why the cops showed up. They said it was the most polite robbery they'd ever seen.
selected_joke:
JOKE 2:
I tried to return something at a store without a receipt. I said "I'm so sorry to bother you, I know this is a hassle, but if it's not too much trouble, could I please have the cash back? Again, I'm really sorry, and thank you so much for your time." They said it was the most polite robbery they'd ever seen.

punchline: Now every time I hear a doorbell, I flinch.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reframes a seemingly normal situation by revealing an absurdly disproportionate trauma response to something mundane, making the audience reconsider what happened in the setup.

---

JOKE 1:
I trained my dog using one of those doorbell cameras. He learned nothing, but I got 400 notifications a day for six months. Now every time I hear a doorbell, I flinch.

---

JOKE 2:
When I was a kid, my mom made me practice piano every day. The rule was I couldn't stop until someone came to the door. So I'd just sit there for hours, praying for a Jehovah's Witness. Eventually one would show up and I'd be free. Then one day my mom got a "No Soliciting" sign. I was on that bench for eleven hours. Now every time I hear a doorbell, I flinch.

---

JOKE 3:
I used to work from home during the pandemic and I'd order everything online. Groceries, clothes, furniture — everything. At one point I was averaging nine deliveries a day. My whole life became just answering the door. I'd be in the shower — doorbell. On a work call — doorbell. Finally asleep at 2 AM — doorbell, because apparently FedEx operates on chaos time. I quit that job, cancelled every subscription, and moved to a cabin with no address. Now every time I hear a doorbell, I flinch.
selected_joke: I trained my dog using one of those doorbell cameras. He learned nothing, but I got 400 notifications a day for six months. Now every time I hear a doorbell, I flinch.

punchline: Which is weird because I don't even own a boat.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals the speaker was never in the expected context where the setup made sense, making everything preceding it absurdly irrelevant.

---

JOKE 1:
My neighbor came over furious, saying I need to keep my dock in better condition because someone could get hurt. He went on for twenty minutes about liability and property values and marine regulations. I just kept nodding. Which is weird because I don't even own a boat.

---

JOKE 2:
I spent four hours last Saturday arguing with my wife about whether we should switch from a two-stroke to a four-stroke outboard motor. It got really heated. She was sleeping on the couch by the end of it. Which is weird because I don't even own a boat.

---

JOKE 3:
I got a letter saying my maritime insurance premium is going up because of three claims I filed last year. I called to dispute it and they put me on hold for forty-five minutes. I was livid. Which is weird because I don't even own a boat.

punchline: He looked me dead in the eye and said, 'Sir, this is a Wendy's.'
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that what seemed like a reasonable or dramatic interaction was actually someone being wildly inappropriate at a fast food restaurant.

---

JOKE 1:
I finally worked up the courage to be vulnerable. I took a deep breath, opened my heart, and told someone my entire five-year plan — the career pivot, the healing journey, the dream of moving to Portugal. He looked me dead in the eye and said, "Sir, this is a Wendy's."

---

JOKE 2:
I've been practicing my negotiation skills. Read three books on it. So when the guy at the counter told me my total was $8.47, I leaned in and said, "Let's not insult each other. We both know that number has some room in it." He looked me dead in the eye and said, "Sir, this is a Wendy's."

---

JOKE 3:
My therapist told me I need to start setting boundaries with people in my life. So the next person who asked me what I wanted, I unloaded. I told him I wanted to feel seen, I wanted to stop performing happiness for others, and I wanted to finally grieve the version of myself I abandoned in my twenties. He looked me dead in the eye and said, "Sir, this is a Wendy's."
selected_joke: I've been practicing my negotiation skills. Read three books on it. So when the guy at the counter told me my total was $8.47, I leaned in and said, "Let's not insult each other. We both know that number has some room in it." He looked me dead in the eye and said, "Sir, this is a Wendy's."

punchline: And I still don't know whose cat that was.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that what the speaker treated as a normal, resolved situation actually contains a massive unresolved question they're weirdly unbothered by.

---

JOKE 1:
I moved into my new apartment, and there was a cat already inside. I just started feeding it. That was six years ago. We've been through two moves, a breakup, and a cross-country road trip together. And I still don't know whose cat that was.

---

JOKE 2:
My veterinarian asked me to fill out some paperwork — breed, age, medical history. I just wrote "unknown" on every line. He said, "How long have you had this cat?" I said, "Well, it showed up at a dinner party I threw in 2018, and everyone assumed it belonged to someone else, and at the end of the night it just stayed." He said, "So it's yours?" I said, "I paid you four thousand dollars for its kidney surgery." And I still don't know whose cat that was.

---

JOKE 3:
The funeral was beautiful. Tasteful little casket. I gave a eulogy. My wife cried. The kids made a memorial collage with photos going back nine years. We buried him under the oak tree in the backyard. Afterward we all sat around sharing our favorite memories, and it hit me — and I still don't know whose cat that was.
selected_joke: The funeral was beautiful. Tasteful little casket. I gave a eulogy. My wife cried. The kids made a memorial collage with photos going back nine years. We buried him under the oak tree in the backyard. Afterward we all sat around sharing our favorite memories, and it hit me — and I still don't know whose cat that was.

punchline: That's when I realized the interview had started twenty minutes ago.
candidates:
REFRAME: The punchline reveals that what the speaker thought was a casual, low-stakes interaction was actually a high-stakes evaluation the entire time — they were being judged without knowing it.

---

JOKE 1:
I showed up early to a job interview and the receptionist was super friendly. We chatted about the weather, my commute, how I got fired from my last job for "poor judgment and a general lack of professionalism." She laughed, I laughed, she wrote something on her clipboard. That's when I realized the interview had started twenty minutes ago.

---

JOKE 2:
I was in the waiting room at a tech company and the guy next to me asked what I thought about their product. I said honestly it's kind of a mess — the UI is unintuitive, the app crashes constantly, and whoever designed it should probably be let go. He thanked me for my candor and extended his hand. "I'm the CEO." That's when I realized the interview had started twenty minutes ago.

---

JOKE 3:
I went in for an interview and they said the hiring manager was running late, so I should just relax and make myself comfortable. So I did. I took my shoes off, microwaved some fish I'd brought, and got into a pretty heated phone argument with my landlord about the meaning of "structural damage." A woman walked over and said, "We've seen enough." That's when I realized the interview had started twenty minutes ago.
selected_joke:
JOKE 3:
I went in for an interview and they said the hiring manager was running late, so I should just relax and make myself comfortable. So I did. I took my shoes off, microwaved some fish I'd brought, and got into a pretty heated phone argument with my landlord about the meaning of "structural damage." A woman walked over and said, "We've seen enough." That's when I realized the interview had started twenty minutes ago.
